channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lightning edge Canadiens 1-0 in overtime to force decisive Game 7 behind Vasilevskiy shutout
item.description: The Tampa Bay Lightning forced a Game 7 in their first-round NHL playoff series against the Montreal Canadiens with a dramatic 1-0 overtime victory in Game 6 at the Bell Centre. Goaltender Andrei Vasi...

item.title: Constitutional Concerns Raised on PM Shehbaz Sharif's Apna Ghar Housing Finance Scheme
item.description: The government of Pakistan has launched the Apna Ghar Programme, a big Rs3.2 trillion plan to finance 500,000 housing units across the country over five years. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif called it ...

item.title: Starmer signals possible limits on protests amid concerns over antisemitism and public safety
item.description: The UK prime minister, Sir Keir Starmer, has indicated that certain protests could potentially be restricted or even halted in response to growing concerns about their cumulative impact, particularly ...

item.title: Alice Springs leaders call for calm after child’s death and hospital unrest
item.description: Alice Springs is grieving after police discovered the body of a five-year-old girl, believed to be the child who had been missing since Sunday. Her family requested that she be referred to as Kumanjay...

item.title: Nara Lokesh appointed TDP working president as Chandrababu Naidu signals leadership transition
item.description: Telugu Desam Party chief and Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu has formally elevated his son, Nara Lokesh, by appointing him as the party’s working president, marking a significant st...

item.title: Kenya’s push to strengthen health systems and build pharmaceutical manufacturing capacity highlighted at World Health Summit in Nairobi
item.description: More than 2,000 delegates from over 50 countries gathered at the United Nations Office in Nairobi for the World Health Summit Regional Meeting, where discussions focused on rethinking Africa’s health ...

item.title: Atin Ito Coalition Observes Labor Day at Sea While En Route to Pag-asa Island in West Philippine Sea
item.description: The Atin Ito Coalition held its fourth civilian mission to the West Philippine Sea and observed Labor Day aboard the MV Kapitan Felix Oca while heading to Pag-asa Island. The group honored Filipino fi...

item.title: China removes import tariffs on Nigerian exports to strengthen trade ties with Nigeria and Africa
item.description: China has announced the removal of import tariffs on goods coming from Nigeria and over 50 other African countries, a move aimed at strengthening trade relations and boosting economic cooperation betw...

item.title: Buffalo Sabres defeat Boston Bruins 4-1 in Game 6 to advance to NHL second round
item.description: The Buffalo Sabres defeated the Boston Bruins 4-1 in Game 6 of their first-round NHL playoff series on Friday night, securing a 4-2 series victory and advancing to the second round for the first time ...

item.title: Why reforms to South Africa’s TVET colleges continue to fail students and employers
item.description: South Africa’s public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) colleges are under severe pressure, with many institutions struggling to deliver strong outcomes for students and employers...

item.title: EU labelling rules under post-Brexit deal may increase costs and reduce honey variety in the UK
item.description: Honey, a popular British breakfast staple enjoyed by King Charles III and a favourite of Winnie the Pooh, could face significant challenges due to new EU regulations included in the UK’s post-Brexit a...

item.title: Debate on CFA Franc Exit and the Path to Senegal’s Economic Transformation
item.description: This article looks into the ongoing debate about whether Senegal should leave the CFA franc system, a currency arrangement used by several West and Central African countries and linked to the euro wit...

item.title: Lawyer says Sara Duterte impeachment will test strength of Philippine democratic institutions
item.description: Sinabi ng isang human rights lawyer na ang impeachment process laban kay Vice President Sara Duterte ay magsisilbing mahalagang pagsubok sa tibay ng demokrasya sa Pilipinas. Ayon kay Atty. Dino de Leo...

item.title: Pete Alonso jokes about NYC traffic and hits home run in return with Orioles against Yankees
item.description: Pete Alonso made his first return to New York City as a visiting player on May 1, 2026, when the Baltimore Orioles faced the New York Yankees in the Bronx. After beginning his MLB career with the New ...

item.title: Four rescued after bus driven by trainee enters River Seine in Paris accident
item.description: According to the available information, an incident occurred in Paris involving a bus that ended up in the River Seine after being driven by a trainee driver. Emergency services responded quickly afte...

item.title: Centre updates citizenship rules and digitises OCI application process
item.description: The Union Home Ministry has notified amendments to the Citizenship Rules, 2009, introducing key changes that impact Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) cardholders and applicants. A major shift in the upd...

item.title: Six Australians freed after Gaza aid flotilla interception near Greece, two leaders still detained
item.description: Six Australian activists detained by the Israeli military while attempting to deliver humanitarian aid to Gaza have been released in Greece following several days at sea. The group was part of the Glo...

item.title: Structural barriers push Nigerian women into home-based work, study shows
item.description: This article explains how working from home for many Nigerian women is not really about flexibility or preference, but more about necessity. The study uses national household data from 2010 to 2019 an...

item.title: Mbeya Rural Electrification Project Reaches 93 Percent Completion Ahead of Schedule
item.description: The rural electrification project in Mbeya Region is now at 93 percent completion, bringing strong hope to more than 3,400 residents who are expected to benefit from access to electricity. The initiat...

item.title: Priscilla Ojo Shares How Iyabo Ojo Raised Her and Her Brother Alone After Their Parents Separated
item.description: Priscilla Ojo, daughter of Nollywood actress Iyabo Ojo, don open up about her childhood and how she and her brother were raised after their parents separated. During a conversation with media personal...
